self_report.numeric_verdict: At a=1 the integral computes to 0.239876 which matches sqrt(pi)*e^{-2}=0.239876 (NOT sqrt(pi)*e^{-1}=0.652049); at a=2 and a=3 the integral equals sqrt(pi/a)*e^{-2a} to ~127 digits, so the exponent should be -2a, not -a.
self_report.notes: Standard substitution u = sqrt(a)(sqrt(t) - 1/sqrt(t)) gives the classical Glasser/Bessel-type identity sqrt(pi/a)*e^{-2a}; the asserted e^{-a} omits a factor of 2 in the exponent.
